title: Manage Negative Recall: - List Positives: After a Bad Experience, Write Down at Least
subtitle: Balance the Negativity Bias
description:
To manage negative recall:
- List positives: After a bad experience, write down at least 3 positive things that happened that day.
- Reframe events: Find the silver lining in unpleasant memories.
- Focus on solutions: Instead of dwelling on what went wrong, brainstorm ways to improve next time.

Example: If a meeting didn’t go well, note what you learned and how you can prepare better next time.

howTo.title: List three positives
howTo.summary: After a negative experience, write down at least three positive things that happened that day. Be specific and concrete to create alternative memory pathways.

howTo.title: Identify a lesson
howTo.summary: Reflect on the negative event and identify one lesson learned. This helps in recognizing what can be improved for future situations.

howTo.title: Plan an immediate action
howTo.summary: Decide on one small action you can take within 15 minutes to address the lesson learned. Schedule this action to enhance accountability.

howTo.title: Review and reflect
howTo.summary: At the end of the week, review your entries to identify patterns and recurring lessons. This helps in understanding your progress and areas for improvement.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and ensure adherence to the practice. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/negativity-bias-positive-reflection/.
